root_text.an1.31:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ adantaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.31:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, adantaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.32:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ dantaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.32:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, dantaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.33:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ aguttaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.33:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, aguttaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.34:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ guttaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.34:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, guttaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.35:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ arakkhitaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.35:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, arakkhitaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.36:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ rakkhitaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.36:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, rakkhitaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.37:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ asaṁvutaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.37:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, asaṁvutaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.38:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ saṁvutaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.38:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, saṁvutaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.39:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ adantaṁ aguttaṁ arakkhitaṁ asaṁvutaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.39:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, adantaṁ aguttaṁ arakkhitaṁ asaṁvutaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.40:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ dantaṁ guttaṁ rakkhitaṁ saṁvutaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.40:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, dantaṁ guttaṁ rakkhitaṁ saṁvutaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.

translation_text.an1.31:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not tamed, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.31:1.2: A wild mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.32:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is tamed, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.32:1.2: A tamed mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.33:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not guarded, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.33:1.2: An unguarded mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.34:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is guarded, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.34:1.2: A guarded mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.35:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not protected, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.35:1.2: An unprotected mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.36:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is protected, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.36:1.2: A protected mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.37:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not restrained, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.37:1.2: An unrestrained mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.38:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is restrained, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.38:1.2: A restrained mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.39:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is not tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.39:1.2: An untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.40:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.40:1.2: A tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained mind is very beneficial.”

reference_text.an1.31:1.1: cck20.7, csp1ed15.6, csp2ed15.6, dr20.7, ms15A1_66, msdiv31.1, vri35.9
